spec_match.rationale: Builds the full attribution_scopes.{values,limit,max_event_states} object with spec-correct validation: 20-scope ceiling, scope_limit >= values.length, max_event_states 1-20, uniqueness.

interactive.rationale: Live editable scope list, numeric bounds, JSON output, live validation list, copy-to-clipboard, trigger match preview — full builder.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises hands-on builder with live spec validation and trigger preview — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Validates four bound cases (empty, over-20, limit<length, duplicates). Misses: scope-string length cap (the spec also constrains individual scope-value byte length), unicode normalisation, trigger.attribution_scopes as an array (only a single string input is supported).

references.rationale: See-also section links to feature page, sibling concepts, and the WICG repo root, but NOT to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5096560068395008 directly. Rule #3 requires the chromestatus link on every page.

design_system.rationale: All variables, copy-btn sets background AND color together, meter accents (emerald/amber/rose) all WCAG-compatible on stone backgrounds.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus.com/feature/<id> to references
openQuestions.detail: The page only links to the WICG repo root and sibling concept pages. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires the chromestatus link explicitly on every concept page — it's the slug-recovery anchor.

openQuestions.title: Validate scope-value byte length and characters
openQuestions.detail: The spec constrains the per-scope string (length cap, allowed characters). Add validation rules for these too so the builder catches a wider class of headers the browser would reject.

openQuestions.title: Support multi-scope triggers in the preview
openQuestions.detail: The trigger-match section only takes a single string. Real triggers can declare an array of scopes; matching is set-intersection. Change the input to a comma-separated list (or chip-builder) and update the verdict to show intersection size.

openQuestions.title: Add an 'invalid header' downloadable example
openQuestions.detail: Once the builder is showing validation errors, expose a copy-button for the broken header too, with a comment explaining the rejection reason. Useful for teams testing their own server's error reporting.
